title: Legal | Piel en Armonia
description: V6 legal framework with clear and operational language for patients in Quito.

terms.heading: Terms and conditions
terms.summary: Conditions for using public clinical routes, telemedicine guidance, and website content.

sections.title: Website usage
items: Informational content does not replace in-person medical care.
You agree to provide truthful information in official channels.
Misuse of the platform may result in restricted access.

sections.title: Intellectual property
items: Text, design, and visual assets belong to Piel en Armonia.
Full reproduction is not authorized without written permission.
Third-party marks appear only as comparative references.

privacy.heading: Privacy policy
privacy.summary: Responsible handling of personal data with minimal exposure principles.

sections.title: Data collected
items: Contact data you share through official channels.
Technical metadata for security and UX improvement.
Clinical information only when you provide it for guidance.

sections.title: Usage and protection
items: Data is used for continuity of care and support operations.
Technical safeguards limit unauthorized access.
You can request correction or deletion under applicable regulation.

cookies.heading: Cookie policy
cookies.summary: Cookie usage for technical features, aggregate analytics, and continuous improvement.

sections.title: Cookie categories
items: Essential cookies for navigation and stability.
Analytics cookies for aggregate route usage measurement.
Preference cookies to remember interface settings.

sections.title: Consent control
items: You may accept or reject non-essential cookies.
Choices can be updated through browser controls.
Rejecting optional cookies does not block core content.

medical-disclaimer.heading: Medical disclaimer
medical-disclaimer.summary: Public information guides decisions but does not replace personalized diagnosis.

sections.title: Limits of digital guidance
items: Public text is not a prescription or final diagnosis.
All treatment decisions require individualized medical review.
Warning signs should be addressed in urgent care settings.

sections.title: Shared responsibility
items: You should report history and symptoms accurately.
The clinical team defines direction based on available evidence.
Adherence to plan influences case evolution.
